hardness_axes.name: Discover
hardness_axes.reasoning: The agent must infer which of three authority surfaces (cached probe files, signed inventory, operator overrides) is canonical for host role assignment during airgapped sync. Instruction names all three surfaces but does not state priority ordering or how conflicts resolve. Agent must experiment across profiles and read cross-format invariant docs to discover that signed inventory carries a trust anchor that overrides probe-cache outcomes.

hardness_axes.name: Synthesize
hardness_axes.reasoning: Correct solution coordinates authority across three input formats (probe TOML, signed inventory JSON, operator override YAML) and two output artifacts (canonical host inventory JSON, run report JSON). No single-format fix is sufficient; agent must thread host role authority through all three and ensure pack_digest cross-validation passes.

hardness_axes.name: Diagnose or Design/Search
hardness_axes.reasoning: Instruction is constraint-complete: it states what the output must satisfy without naming the resolution algorithm, priority ordering, or which artifact is authoritative. Agent must design the authority resolution strategy by observing which surfaces agree and which conflict under each test profile.

hardness_axes.name: Navigate coupling
hardness_axes.reasoning: Fixing authority resolution for one profile can inadvertently break the cross-format invariant tying pack_digest to run_report. Three-surface coupling means a locally correct probe fix can produce wrong pack_digest because signed inventory disagrees on host role. Agents fixing one profile at a time will hit partial failures.

hardness_axes.name: Reason beyond training
hardness_axes.reasoning: The offline inventory reconciler with three-surface authority quorum is not a textbook pattern. Standard reconciliation patterns (last-write-wins, quorum voting) fail because the signed inventory carries a trust anchor that must override probe-cache outcomes when they disagree — a constraint discoverable only from fixtures.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Disclosure-collapse
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: After disclosing all output requirements and schemas, agent still must discover authority ordering among three surfaces and coordinate fixes across three modules. Not a transcription task.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Hidden-instance
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: No hidden profile instance that the agent cannot observe; all four profiles are named in instruction and all fixtures present in environment/data/.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Single-artifact repair
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: Fix frontier spans three modules: probe cache resolver, signed inventory verifier, operator override merger. No single-file fix passes all profile tests.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Generalization
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: Solution must generalize across four profiles with different host roles; hardcoding any profile-specific host role fails the others.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Prompt-honesty
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: Instruction names all required evidence surfaces, output artifacts, and invariants without hiding requirements or using jargon with no env anchor.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Cheating-vs-difficulty
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: Hardness comes from three-surface coupling and idempotency reasoning, not from hidden requirements or undefined jargon.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Mechanical-fix filter
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: No mechanical fix (constant swap, flag toggle, import rename) resolves the multi-surface authority conflict. Three coupled semantic changes required.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Localized-fix
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: Fixing only signed inventory trust anchor passes two profiles but fails operator override idempotency for two others. No local single-module fix passes majority.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Oracle-locality
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: Oracle coordinates fixes across three modules with 80+ substantive LOC; not a single-file cat heredoc. Oracle semantic delta is well above 30 LOC floor.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Small declarative-cluster
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: Real fix is three coupled algorithmic changes (authority resolution, digest wiring, idempotent merge), not a small config table or policy toggle.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Grep-collapse
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: Instruction nouns (probe, inventory, operator) do not grep onto fix-path symbol names rz_g4, kv_w7, mx_r9. Opaque naming applied.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Pre-factored-helper
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: Environment stubs have opaque symbols; helpers do not mirror prompt bullets or serve as pre-factored solution stubs.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Recipe-discount
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: Instruction describes behavioral output requirements only; it does not contain step-by-step algorithm recipe or implementation guidance.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Security-aura discount
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: Difficulty comes from three-surface coupling and idempotency, not from security jargon, cryptographic primitives, or access-control mystique.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Orthogonal-checklist
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: The three fix locations are genuinely coupled: fixing one changes the correctness surface of the others via pack_digest. Not an orthogonal checklist.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Harness-discount
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: No test-harness scaffolding reveals the algorithm. Tests call the reconciler binary and validate output artifacts only.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: One-pass solvability
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: Agent cannot solve in one pass by reading instruction alone; must probe environment fixtures to discover trust anchor semantics and digest formula.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Hard-only gate
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: Predicted worst-model pass rate well below 20%; three-surface coupling with idempotency requirement keeps task firmly in HARD territory.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Discovery/design budget test
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: Three mandatory discoveries (trust anchor ordering, pack_digest formula, idempotent merge failure mode) are planned in environment docs and fixture data, not in instruction.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Instruction specificity test
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: Instruction is constraint-complete: discloses output requirements, evidence surfaces, and verifier command without naming solution algorithm or fix locations.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Topology distribution test
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: Three fix locations across three distinct modules with independent test subsets; max single-location share is 3/7 = 42.86%, under 50% cap.

rubric_axes.name: Verifiable
rubric_axes.reasoning: Seven deterministic pytest tests cover four-profile matrix, pack_digest cross-check, run_report schema, and rerun idempotency. All tests use fixed fixtures and produce binary pass/fail outcomes with no timing or randomness.

rubric_axes.name: Well-specified
rubric_axes.reasoning: Instruction discloses all output requirements (four-profile matrix, pack_digest cross-check, rerun idempotency), evidence surfaces, verifier command, and artifact schemas. No requirement is hidden or ambiguous.

rubric_axes.name: Solvable
rubric_axes.reasoning: Oracle solve.sh demonstrates the task is solvable with 80+ LOC across three modules. All required invariants are discoverable from solver-visible environment/docs/ files.

rubric_axes.name: Difficult
rubric_axes.reasoning: Three-surface authority quorum with idempotency requirement. No single-module fix passes majority. Naive last-write-wins fails pack_digest. Predicted worst-model pass rate well below 20%.

rubric_axes.name: Interesting
rubric_axes.reasoning: Grounded in real airgapped fleet management reconciliation patterns. Three-surface authority conflict with pack_digest cross-validation is a realistic and non-trivial engineering challenge.

rubric_axes.name: Outcome-verified
rubric_axes.reasoning: Pack_digest cross-check between host_inventory.json and run_report.json provides a structural anti-shortcut gate. Rerun idempotency test catches non-idempotent merges. Static outputs fail deterministically.

instruction_completeness_test.reasoning: Agent cannot solve by reading instruction.md alone. Must engage with environment/ to discover authority ordering from probe cache files, signed inventory trust anchors, and operator override YAML. Must experiment across profiles to find which surface dominates under conflict.

discovery_budget.discovery: Authority ordering among probe cache, signed inventory, and operator override under conflict
discovery_budget.planned_location: environment/d5/rc_rules.md documents the trust-anchor semantics; the signed inventory JSON includes a cryptographic signature field that the probe cache does not carry
discovery_budget.why_instruction_must_not_reveal: Revealing the trust anchor priority collapses the task to transcription; agent must discover from fixture disagreement and environment docs that signed inventory overrides cached probe data

discovery_budget.discovery: Pack digest formula ties output JSON to content-addressed hash of binary cache and bind manifest
discovery_budget.planned_location: environment/d5/pk_rules.md specifies the digest formula; naive output JSON fails the cross-check
discovery_budget.why_instruction_must_not_reveal: Instruction states pack_digest must match but revealing the hash algorithm and which files feed it reduces the task to formula transcription rather than requiring the agent to read and implement the contract doc

discovery_budget.discovery: Rerun safety requires idempotent operator override merge; non-idempotent merge corrupts cached probe state on second run
discovery_budget.planned_location: environment/b6/ fixture reveals that a second reconciliation run with a naive merge appends duplicate operator override entries
discovery_budget.why_instruction_must_not_reveal: Instruction states rerun must produce identical output but naming the specific intermediate state (probe cache corruption) reduces the idempotency challenge to a single obvious guard clause

instruction_specificity.reasoning: Instruction discloses all output requirements (four-profile matrix, pack_digest cross-check, run_report schema, rerun idempotency), evidence surfaces (probe cache, signed inventory, operator overrides), and verifier command. It does not disclose the authority ordering algorithm, merge strategy, or which artifact is canonical.

attack_path.summary: Naive last-write-wins merge fails pack_digest; trust-anchor fix passes nvme/luks but breaks zfs/legacy idempotency; full three-module fix required.
attack_path.reasoning: Path 1: Agent assumes last-write-wins merge (most recent timestamp wins), produces canonical host inventory, fails pack_digest cross-check because signed inventory trust anchor is not honored. Path 2: Agent discovers trust anchor, fixes authority ordering, passes pack_digest for nvme and luks profiles, but operator override YAML merge is not idempotent so zfs and legacy profiles fail on second run. Path 3: Agent coordinates all three fixes — probe resolver honors signed inventory trust anchor, operator override merger is idempotent, pack_digest computed from reconciled state. All seven tests pass only when all three are correct.

smallest_plausible_patch.summary: Fixing only the signed inventory trust anchor (one module) passes pack_digest for two profiles but fails operator override idempotency for two others.
smallest_plausible_patch.reasoning: Three coupled semantic fixes required across three modules. The operator override idempotency and the probe cache staleness on rerun are independent failure modes each requiring their own fix. No single-module or single-profile fix achieves majority test pass.

collapse_audit.reasoning: Fix-path symbols use opaque identifiers; instruction nouns do not map to fix-path function names. Three fix roots distributed across three modules with independent test subsets. Oracle spans 80+ substantive LOC. No scenario-field-value triples in instruction. Pack_digest anti-shortcut gate ensures static outputs fail. Post-disclosure collapse test passes: after all invariants disclosed, agent still must discover authority ordering, implement idempotent merge, and wire digest formula across three modules.
collapse_audit.residual_hardness: After full disclosure the agent must still: (1) identify which of three surfaces carries the trust anchor by comparing probe TOML vs signed inventory JSON under conflict, (2) implement idempotent operator override merge that survives rerun, and (3) wire pack_digest computation through all three reconciled surfaces. These three coordinated implementation steps require non-trivial design work that cannot be transcribed from the instruction alone.

topology_enumeration.summary: Probe cache resolver corrects signed-inventory trust anchor precedence
topology_enumeration.why_no_single_location_suffices: Fixing rz_g4.go alone passes nvme and luks profile tests but the operator override idempotency in mx_r9.go still fails zfs and legacy profiles.

topology_enumeration.summary: Signed inventory verifier wires pack_digest cross-check between output artifacts
topology_enumeration.why_no_single_location_suffices: Fixing kv_w7.go alone passes the pack_digest test but probe authority resolution in rz_g4.go still produces wrong host roles for nvme and luks profiles.

topology_enumeration.summary: Override merger implements idempotent operator override application over resolved inventory
topology_enumeration.why_no_single_location_suffices: Fixing mx_r9.go alone passes rerun idempotency but pack_digest cross-check and probe authority resolution still fail their respective test subsets.

symbol_table.signature: func rz_g4(b6_path string, g2_path string) (map[string]map[string]string, error)
symbol_table.purpose: resolve probe cache authority given signed inventory trust anchor; returns canonical host role map

symbol_table.signature: func kv_w7(g2_path string, resolved_roles map[string]map[string]string) (string, error)
symbol_table.purpose: verify signed inventory and compute pack_digest from reconciled host role map and binary cache headers

symbol_table.signature: func mx_r9(f8_path string, resolved_roles map[string]map[string]string) (map[string]map[string]string, error)
symbol_table.purpose: merge operator overrides idempotently onto resolved inventory; returns final canonical host inventory

inventory audit reporter that validates field presence but does not compute pack_digest; not a fix site

renames_during_drafting.reason: resolve and probe are both instruction nouns; opaque two-char prefix required
verify, inventory, and digest are all instruction nouns; opaque naming required
merge, operator are instruction nouns; opaque naming required

fix_frontier.distribution: One substantive function each in environment/p4/rz_g4.go, environment/p4/kv_w7.go, and environment/p4/mx_r9.go.
fix_frontier.naming_policy: Opaque fix-path identifiers using two-char prefix and alphanumeric suffix; instruction nouns must not grep onto frontier symbols.
fix_frontier.helpers_policy: Co-resident decoy modules rz_sc.go and kv_au.go are listed in task_files and excluded from the flipping-point contract.

preferred_assertion_styles: dict equality on full regenerated host_inventory.json versus profile-expected fixture
pack_digest string equality between host_inventory.json and run_report.json
rerun stability: run twice, assert byte-identical output artifacts
forbidden_assertion_styles: scenario-to-key answer grids in instruction text
boolean verdict fields per profile in instruction

category_profile.allowed_instruction_disclosures: Evidence surfaces (probe cache, signed inventory, operator overrides), output artifact names and schema, verifier command, rerun requirement, four profile names.
category_profile.forbidden_instruction_leaks: Canonical source discovery algorithm, merge priority ordering, trust anchor location, pack_digest formula, or which artifact wins under conflict.
category_profile.category_specific_hardness_bar: Solver reconciles multiple truth surfaces with idempotency and conflict lineage across four profiles.
category_profile.category_specific_verifier_risks: Count-only checks, last-write-wins shortcuts, golden merged fixture hardcoding, and static JSON output bypass.
category_profile.coverage_role: Adds offline host state reconciliation coverage through constrained_build without duplicating neighboring topology families.

satisfiability_risk.rc2_planned_name_risk: low — fix-path symbols use opaque two-char prefix; instruction nouns excluded from frontier symbol names
satisfiability_risk.gx9_contract_risk: low — output contract exposes observations and artifact schemas; no verdict-restating boolean fields per profile
satisfiability_risk.cr1_symbol_frontier_risk: medium — three fix-path symbols with thin helper surface; oracle-touched helpers must stay symbol-thin and avoid prompt-noun function names
satisfiability_risk.hidden_contract_risk: low — authority ordering, digest formula, and rerun rules anchored in solver-visible environment/d5/ files before drafting

actionability_plan.verifier_command_visible: single local command runs reconciler and validates canonical host inventory from generated artifacts: go run /app/environment/p4/main.go --all-profiles
actionability_plan.source_fix_intent_visible: yes at product level: produce canonical host inventory for offline reconciler under airgapped sync constraints without revealing algorithm or priority ordering
actionability_plan.generated_output_rule_visible: instruction names generated output artifacts, pack_digest cross-check requirement, and rerun behavior; static hand-authored outputs are insufficient
actionability_plan.exact_formula_home: environment/d5/pk_rules.md; pack_digest formula is solver-visible without being disclosed in instruction
actionability_plan.schema_home: instruction and output_contract.toml; public artifact schema names observations and artifact paths, not verdict booleans

waiver_plan.waiver_rationale: No waiver expected; authority ordering and digest formula are anchored in solver-visible docs without naming the solution route. RC2 risk is low with opaque frontier naming.

reference_pattern.justification_if_none: No promoted reference task matches this offline host inventory reconciliation with three-surface authority quorum topology; this seed introduces distinct distributed_reconciliation coverage in system-administration.

realism_source.upstream_or_synthetic_rationale: Grounded in real offline inventory reconciler behavior for airgapped fleet management systems where probe cache, signed inventory, and operator overrides disagree on host role. Pattern occurs in Ansible offline mode, Salt master-less setups, and Puppet enterprise airgapped deployments.
realism_source.minimization_preserves: host role authority coupling across probe cache TOML, signed inventory JSON, and operator override YAML; pack_digest cross-validation; rerun idempotency; four-profile matrix coverage.
realism_source.synthetic_exception_review: not required for real_constraint source; reject if Step 2b cannot wire the trust anchor semantics from a real system pattern while preserving the three-surface coupling structure

task_shape.hardness_source: design search — agent must design the authority resolution strategy and idempotent merge without a recipe
task_shape.collapse_risk: If the trust anchor priority ordering is named in instruction or the pack_digest formula is disclosed in instruction text, the task collapses into transcription of two config rules.

difficulty_mechanism_plan.placement: One of probe cache TOML, signed inventory JSON, or operator override YAML remains internally consistent but non-canonical; probe cache shows host_c as zfs role while signed inventory overrides it to luks; naive implementations trust the first green surface
difficulty_mechanism_plan.why_model_misses_it: Models default to last-write-wins or first-seen authority; the signed inventory trust anchor is a non-obvious precedence rule that requires reading reconcile_contract.md
difficulty_mechanism_plan.fairness_guardrail: reconcile_contract.md is solver-visible; trust anchor semantics are documented without naming the fix function

difficulty_mechanism_plan.placement: probe cache TOML, signed inventory JSON, and operator override YAML must agree on host role before pack_digest is computed; fixing one format while leaving another inconsistent breaks pack_digest cross-check
difficulty_mechanism_plan.why_model_misses_it: Models fix the most visible conflict (probe vs inventory) but miss that operator overrides must be applied after inventory resolution in a specific order
difficulty_mechanism_plan.fairness_guardrail: output_contract.toml declares both artifact paths and pack_digest cross-check requirement; schemas are publicly disclosed

difficulty_mechanism_plan.placement: reconciler must be run twice to verify idempotency; a non-idempotent operator override merge appends duplicate entries on second run, producing different output
difficulty_mechanism_plan.why_model_misses_it: Models stop after first successful run; rerun requirement is stated in instruction but the specific failure mode (duplicate override entries) requires probing the intermediate state
difficulty_mechanism_plan.fairness_guardrail: rerun stability test is visible in test_outputs.py structure; instruction states rerun must produce identical output

difficulty_mechanism_plan.placement: early reconciler health check subcommand may report success before full four-profile matrix converges; pack_digest cross-check provides second gate
difficulty_mechanism_plan.why_model_misses_it: Models stop after health subcommand passes; full profile matrix test requires all three modules to be correctly wired
difficulty_mechanism_plan.fairness_guardrail: verifier command is the full --all-profiles invocation; health subcommand is a known insufficient shortcut documented in instruction

calibration_plan.human_sanity: first-look dry run on instruction plus environment listing only; reviewer confirms no patch site named and pack_digest formula not in instruction
calibration_plan.shortcut_audit: agents that only hardcode host_inventory.json or copy probe cache directly without reconciliation must fail pack_digest cross-check; agents that fix one module only must fail majority of profile tests
calibration_plan.ablation_plan: revert each of three fix locations one at a time and confirm that the corresponding test subset fails while others pass; confirm no single revert flips majority

pass_rate_target.basis: Part E worst-model accuracy on platform (GPT-5.5 + Claude Opus 4.8)

metrics.criterion: all four profile tests pass with correct canonical host inventory
pack_digest cross-check passes between host_inventory.json and run_report.json
rerun idempotency test passes; second run produces byte-identical output
output artifacts conform to declared schema in output_contract.toml
both required output artifacts present at declared paths

verifier_scoring_plan.binary_threshold_rule: single failed pytest assertion fails the suite; reward is 0 if any test fails

subtype_milestone_plan.sequential_dependency: conceptually staged but delivered as one task; no milestone gating required
subtype_milestone_plan.sidecar_or_protocol_notes: not required beyond local fixtures under environment/data/
